task: I need a short reply I can send back to a vendor about an invoice issue. Can you draft something I can send as-is, with a blank where I can drop in the exact number or reference?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short version you can send as-is:

> Invoice [#] does not match the order record. Please confirm the correct [amount / item / quantity / date] in writing so I can update my records. I’m not moving forward until the paperwork matches.

If you want, I can make it **firmer** or **more formal**.

user_simulator.text: Close, but I need it a bit tighter and more specific. The first line is fine, but I want it to state the invoice issue without the extra options in brackets, and I want the blank kept for the exact number/reference. Also, don’t leave it sounding like a conversation invite. Just give me one clean version I can send.

turns.agent_response: Invoice [#] does not match the order record. Confirm the correct [detail] in writing so I can update my records. I will not move forward until the paperwork matches.

user_simulator.text:
Better, but still a little generic. I need it to read like a real invoice dispute, not a placeholder. Keep the blank for the invoice number/reference, but make the issue itself specific enough to use without tweaking.

Something like:

Invoice ____ does not match the order record. Please confirm the correct amount in writing so I can update my records.

Just one clean version like that.

turns.agent_response: Invoice [#] does not match the order record. Please confirm the correct amount in writing so I can update my records. I will not move forward until the paperwork matches.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. Still a little stiff. Make it sound like an actual billing dispute, not a template.

Invoice ____ does not match the order record. Please confirm the correct amount in writing so I can update my records.

If you can, tighten it one step more and keep it blunt.

turns.agent_response: Invoice ____ does not match the order record. Confirm the correct amount in writing so I can update my records.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s close. Still a little too clean. Make it sound like a real dispute — maybe "Invoice ____ does not match the order record. Please confirm the correct amount in writing so I can update my records." That’s the level I want. Blunt, no fluff.

turns.agent_response: Invoice ____ does not match the order record. Please confirm the correct amount in writing so I can update my records.
